title: Sciodaphyllum clausum
intro: Sciodaphyllum clausum, also known as the Puerto Rican Laurel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sciodaphyllum clausum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to relieve respiratory issues and reduce fever.
